memory.summary: I just saw the quiz is due Thursday 09:00, and the team is still meeting at 19:00 today, but Assignment A is still only half done. With my low energy and big sleep debt, I feel extra stressed about timing.
With my energy crashing and sleep debt piling up, everything already feels tighter—like the remaining work on Assignment A might not fit the timeline, and that makes my stress spike more than usual.

Teammate_A’s message about whether the meeting materials are ready makes it clear they’re expecting me to deliver soon, and with my heavy sleep debt and low energy, I’m feeling even more pressured to finish quickly.

Nothing new came in, but my energy is wiped out and my sleep debt is huge, so the existing quiz and Assignment A timeline feels even tighter. I’m more stressed than before because I can’t rely on stamina right now.

My energy is completely gone and my sleep debt is enormous, so the same Assignment A timeline now feels basically unworkable. Even without new info, I’m more pressured than before because I can’t “push through” like usual.

desires: Don’t look unreliable to Teammate_A—like if I’m late on materials or the meeting stuff, it’ll confirm they should’ve counted on someone else.
Make tomorrow morning (Thursday 9AM quiz) feel survivable—so I don’t have to gamble on willpower when my energy is basically zero.
Avoid the feeling that Assignment A is “unworkable”—I really want to regain control of the timeline, even if it means cutting scope a bit.
Get relief from stress quickly by finishing one concrete piece fast, because I can’t tolerate that spiraling pressure while exhausted.

goals.description: Finish Individual assignment A to a safe “submitted okay” baseline: complete remaining unclear/missing parts, do a quick formatting pass, then one final 5-minute skim for obvious breakage before submitting.
goals.rationale: This is the only hard deadline tonight, and it’s the quickest way to stop the stress spiral while protecting my reliability with teammate_A.

goals.description: Quiz prep minimum viable path: spend a focused block skimming key topics for the Thu 9AM quiz, then do 2–3 representative problems. End by writing a short “what to remember” cheat sheet for tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: With my energy basically zero, I need tomorrow to feel survivable—this gives me an early buffer without betting everything on willpower.

goals.description: Send teammate_A a clear reliability update: what’s done, what’s left on my materials, and one concrete question I’ll bring to the meeting (short + specific).
goals.rationale: Directly addresses my fear of looking unreliable, while staying in the compromise lane (no overcommitting).

goals.description: Protect capacity while working Assignment A + quiz prep: take a 15–20 min reset mid-session (water/food/stretch), then enforce a hard stop-time for tonight so I actually recover.
goals.rationale: If I ignore sleep/work capacity, everything becomes “unworkable.” This is how I regain control of the timeline.
